description_long: This object is a trapezoidal concrete jersey barrier, commonly used for traffic control and safety in urban environments. Its surface is textured with visible cracks and roughness, giving it a weathered, utilitarian appearance. Two rusted metal lifting hooks are embedded on the top, allowing for mechanical handling and placement. The sides taper slightly inward from the base to the top, characteristic of standard jersey barrier design for deflecting vehicles. The concrete is a mottled gray, with darker veins and patches that suggest exposure to the elements. There are no visible markings, reflective strips, or warning signs on its surface.
description: A weathered gray concrete jersey barrier with rusted metal lifting hooks and a rough, cracked surface.
description_view_0: Front view showing the wide, flat face of the barrier with two rusted metal hooks on top.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the trapezoidal profile and the depth of the barrier, with hooks visible on the top edge.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, displaying the flat surface and both metal hooks.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the trapezoidal shape and the hooks on top.
